title: fabric-archive SKILL.md — Phase 0.4 onboard trigger gate (E2-only execution)
description: Wrap the existing Phase 0.4 onboard coverage flow with an entry-context gate. Onboard slot prompting only runs when the skill was invoked via E2 (user explicit `/fabric-archive`); E1 (hook-passive), E3 (AI self-trigger), E4 (user range回溯), E5 (cron-daily) skip Phase 0.4 entirely. Resolves the UX错位 where hook/AI/cron contexts would otherwise be interrupted by an interactive onboard wizard.

files.change:
(1) Insert a new H4 'Phase 0.4 Trigger Gate' at the top of the Phase 0.4 section (between '### Phase 0.4 — First-run Onboard Phase (rc.23 F8c)' header and 'After F8a removed...' paragraph). (2) Gate logic: Read invocation context (already determined in Phase -0.5 from TASK-04). If context.entry_point ∈ {E2_explicit_user_invoke, E4_user_range_rollback (E2 sub-mode)} → proceed to existing Phase 0.4 flow. Otherwise (E1_hook, E3_ai_self_trigger, E5_cron) → SKIP Phase 0.4 entirely and go directly to Phase 0. (3) Document the entry-context detection rules:
  - E1: stdout JSON {decision:'block'} from archive-hint.cjs detected at skill entry
  - E2: user prompt is a direct invocation like 'fabric archive' / '/fabric-archive' / 'archive what we just did'
  - E3: AI内部 'self-archive policy triggered by signal X' marker
  - E4: prompt contains range hint (parsed in Phase -0.5) AND user is invoking
  - E5: prompt contains '今日复盘' / 'daily recap' literal AND no human present (running under /loop or cron context)
(4) Add gate-skip rationale paragraph: 'Onboard slot collection is a one-time project-tone capture flow that requires user dialogue. Non-user-active entries (hook/AI/cron) interrupt the user mid-work or run unattended where dialogue is impossible, so they MUST skip Phase 0.4.'

implementation: Insert the new 'Phase 0.4 Trigger Gate' subsection at the top.
Write the entry-context detection table (5 entries with detection rules each).
Add the rationale paragraph explaining why non-user-active entries must skip.
Cross-reference TASK-04 Phase -0.5 for entry-context determination.
Verify the rest of Phase 0.4 (Step 1 Check coverage / Step 2 Decide / Step 3 Prompt / Step 4 Tour-and-propose) is unchanged.
Add a worked example: E5 cron invokes /fabric-archive 今日复盘 → entry_point=E5 → Phase 0.4 skip → proceed to Phase 0.

reference.pattern: Existing Phase 0.5 viability gate (L312-L351) — gate-PASS vs gate-FAIL pattern with explicit decision logic
reference.examples: Phase 0.5 gate logic is the structural model for Phase 0.4 trigger gate.

rationale.chosen_approach: Pure SKILL.md prose change. AI reads the gate logic at skill entry and decides skip-vs-execute. Entry-context detection is LLM-native (reading prompt + presence/absence of hook JSON marker).
alternatives_considered: --no-onboard internal flag passed by hook (rejected: violates 'no flags' principle)
Phase 0.4 fully auto mode that doesn't prompt (rejected: defeats onboard's purpose of capturing user-validated project tone)
Skip Phase 0.4 for ALL invocations (rejected: E2 first-time user genuinely needs onboard prompt)
decision_factors: E2 is the only natural context for interactive prompting
Hook/cron/AI contexts have no human available
Pure SKILL.md keeps the boundary in policy not code
rationale.tradeoffs: First-time users who only ever trigger via hook (never explicit invoke) won't see the onboard prompt; the 5 onboard slots remain empty. Mitigation: documentation in CHANGELOG + SKILL.md noting 'explicit fab archive at least once to populate onboard slots'.

manual_checks: Dogfood: trigger fabric-archive via hook in a clean workspace (no onboard yet) → verify Phase 0.4 skipped, no AskUserQuestion fired
Dogfood: explicit /fabric-archive in same workspace → verify Phase 0.4 prompts onboard slots
success_metrics: SKILL.md Phase 0.4 has new H4 'Phase 0.4 Trigger Gate'
integration: Manual dogfood validates 2-context behavior (hook-skip vs explicit-run)

criteria: SKILL.md Phase 0.4 contains exact string 'Phase 0.4 Trigger Gate'
SKILL.md contains entry-context table with E1, E2, E3, E4, E5 detection rules
SKILL.md contains exact string 'Non-user-active entries' OR 'non-user-active entries'
SKILL.md contains the rationale paragraph about interrupting user mid-work
Commit msg: 'feat(rc25): Phase 0.4 onboard trigger gate (E2-only) (TASK-06)'
